summary: 8 integration acceptance tests written in internal/acceptance/swarmforge_integration_test.go — ALL RED (compilation fails). Tests demand: expanded start.Config, full start.Run() sequence, tmux.NewExecCommander, and cmd/swarmforge/main.go.
instructions:
8 integration tests are RED in internal/acceptance/swarmforge_integration_test.go. They fail to compile. Follow TDD to make them pass. Here's exactly what must change:

## 1. Expand start.Config (internal/start/start.go)
Add these fields:
  - FS               setup.FS
  - LookPath         preflight.LookPathFunc
  - ConstitutionPath string
  - Stdout           io.Writer

Import setup, preflight, io packages.

## 2. Expand start.Run() to perform FULL sequence:
  a. preflight.Check(cfg.LookPath, "tmux", "claude", "watch") — fail fast if error
  b. setup.EnsureDirs(cfg.FS, cfg.ProjectRoot)
  c. setup.WriteHelperScripts(cfg.FS, cfg.ProjectRoot)
  d. Read constitution: cfg.FS.ReadFile(cfg.ProjectRoot + "/" + cfg.ConstitutionPath)
     - If error, return fmt.Errorf wrapping 'constitution' in the message
  e. banner.Print(cfg.Stdout)
  f. Kill existing session if cfg.Commander.HasSession(cfg.Session)
  g. tmux.CreateSession, SplitGrid, SetPaneTitles
  h. Generate 3 agent prompts and write to cfg.FS:
     - Architect: prompt.Build(AgentConfig{Role:"Architect", Instructions:prompt.ArchitectInstructions, Session:cfg.Session, ProjectRoot:cfg.ProjectRoot}, constitution)
     - E2E-Interpreter: same with E2EInterpreterInstructions
     - Coder: same with CoderInstructions
     - Write each to: cfg.ProjectRoot + "/.swarmforge/prompts/" + name + ".md"
     - Must MkdirAll the prompts dir first
  i. tmux.LaunchAgent for panes 0 (Architect), 1 (E2E-Interpreter), 2 (Coder)
     - promptFile = cfg.ProjectRoot + "/.swarmforge/prompts/" + name + ".md"
  j. tmux.SendKeys(cmd, session, "swarm", 3, "cd '" + projectRoot + "' && touch logs/agent_messages.log && tail -f logs/agent_messages.log")

IMPORTANT: The existing TestCLI_StartKillsExistingSession test passes start.Config WITHOUT the new fields. You must make the new fields have sensible zero-value behavior OR update that test. Recommended: check the existing test in swarmforge_cli_test.go and update it to pass the new required fields.

## 3. Create tmux.NewExecCommander() (internal/tmux/exec.go)
  - ExecCommander struct wrapping os/exec
  - Run(args ...string) error — exec.Command("tmux", args...).Run()
  - HasSession(name string) bool — exec.Command("tmux", "has-session", "-t", name).Run() == nil
  - NewExecCommander() *ExecCommander
  - Must satisfy tmux.Commander interface

## 4. Create cmd/swarmforge/main.go
  - package main with func main()
  - Create real ExecCommander, real FS (os-backed), exec.LookPath as LookPath
  - Wire cli.Dispatch with:
    - "start" → start.Run with full Config
    - "notify" → notify.Notify
    - "log" → swarmlog logger.Write
  - Must build: go build ./cmd/swarmforge/

## 5. Update existing TestCLI_StartKillsExistingSession
In swarmforge_cli_test.go, the test at line ~220 creates start.Config with only Commander/Session/ProjectRoot. Add the new required fields (FS, LookPath, ConstitutionPath, Stdout) so it compiles. Use a FakeFS with a constitution file, a passing lookPath, and a bytes.Buffer for Stdout.

Run: go test ./internal/acceptance/ -v to verify ALL tests pass (integration + CLI + original).
